title: App Engine: Qwik Start – Python & Flask
aim: To deploy a Python web application to Google App Engine using standard and flexible environments.

steps.title: Part A: App Engine Standard
steps.description: Deploy a simple app using the standard environment.

steps.title: Enable App Engine Admin API
steps.description: Go to APIs & Services > Library. Search for and enable 'App Engine Admin API'.

steps.title: Download and Test App
steps.description: Clone the sample repo, navigate to the hello_world folder, and run 'dev_appserver.py' to test locally.

steps.title: Deploy the App
steps.description: Run 'gcloud app deploy' and choose a region to deploy the app to the cloud.

steps.title: Part B: App Engine Flexible
steps.description: Deploy a Flask app using the flexible environment.

steps.title: Deploy Flexible App
steps.description: Clone the flexible sample, review app.yaml (env: flex), and run 'gcloud app deploy'.

commands.purpose: Deploy your application to App Engine
Open the deployed application in your browser
Run the App Engine app locally for testing

viva.question: Difference between App Engine Standard and Flexible?
viva.answer: Standard is for specific languages with fast scaling and scaling to zero. Flexible supports custom runtimes via Docker and is better for apps with consistent traffic.

viva.question: What is app.yaml?
viva.answer: It is the configuration file for App Engine that defines the runtime, environment, and handlers for the application.

notes: App Engine Standard is for Python/Java/Go/Node apps with fast scaling. Flexible supports custom runtimes via Docker containers.
